strategy: Never join late. Latecomers are hit by a Star Insane Bolt for 75% of max health. Enter together before the health bars appear so the shield and bolt cheats do not double up.

Survive the one-time Primary quiz cycle. He polymorphs the whole team, scales your health to a share of 10,000, and locks you out of your own spells - you can only play the quiz cards he hands you. He does not attack during this cycle as long as you answer correctly.

Roll Call (attendance): every wizard plays the Attendance card equal to the party size (all four play Attendance (4) in a full team). A wrong count or a pass is a Star Crow for 10% max health; playing Fake Attendance is a Star Immolate for 35%.

Homework: play your own school's Homework card to turn it in - this both answers the round and breaks his -999% shields. Using Copy costs 15% max health, and the second-position wizard skipping homework costs 20%.

Lecture: pick a study buff like Participate or Take Notes. Avoid Snack Break (auto-fails your next quiz) and Doze Off (Leprechaun + Cyclops for 55% total). A pet-cast Conviction beforehand helps dodge the stun.

Pop Quiz: he puts a school Aura on you and you play the OPPOSING school card (Death aura -> Life, Fire -> Ice, Storm -> Myth, and so on). A right answer removes the aura free; a wrong one is a Star Supernova for 40% max health.

Break his shields and burst. He self-shields -999% for all seven schools, so clear them with Pierce and the Homework turns. Once the cycle ends you get your spells back - hit for at least 10,000 damage together. He resists Ice/Storm/Death 70% and heals at 45%, so attack with Fire/Myth/Life or a huge Balance hit (75% boost).

Watch the Secondary Homework cycle: mid-fight he can interrupt for two rounds with normal cards and attacks. Use those turns to break more shields, but strip any Shadow Creature/Shadow Self first - the polymorph removes them for 4,000 backlash.
cheatNotes: Any late wizard is interrupt-hit by a Star Insane Bolt for 75% of current max health, so everyone must be in before combat starts.
He runs a one-time Primary quiz cycle at the start (no attacks) and can later drop shorter Secondary Homework cycles where he does attack.
During the primary cycle he will not attack the team at all, so the only danger is answering the quizzes wrong.
He self-casts -999% shields for every one of the seven schools, so ordinary attacks do nothing until you strip them.
If someone joins after the health bars appear, the seven-school shield cheat fires twice on the first round.
Hitting a shield with a damage spell just makes him re-shield that one broken school, so do not try to burn shields off with attacks.
Use Pierce and the Homework turns, not damage, to actually remove his shields.
His single Balance shield shows up looking like a Tower Shield.
Casting Shatter makes him recast all seven -999% shields, so leave Shatter out of the deck.
Casting Steal Ward fails - he immediately casts a Star Steal Ward to take the shield back.
Casting Double Steal Ward is also countered by a Star version that steals both back.
Casting Empower gives him an extra -999% Death shield, so avoid Empower.
He casts an invisible polymorph on the whole team, replacing your deck with quiz cards.
Your health is temporarily scaled to a fraction of 10,000 during the cycle, proportional to what you entered with.
You cannot cast your own spells until the primary cycle ends - only the quiz cards work.
When the cycle ends the polymorph drops and your real health is restored proportionally.
This full primary cycle happens only once, at the very start of the duel.
Any Treasure Cards you draw while polymorphed are stuck and cannot be discarded.
For Roll Call, each wizard selects the total number of wizards in the duel by playing the matching Attendance card.
Using Draw during Roll Call hands you Fake Attendance - do not play it.
Passing during Roll Call costs 10% max health to a Star Crow.
Choosing the wrong attendance count also costs 10% max health to a Star Crow.
Playing Fake Attendance is punished with a Star Immolate for 35% max health.
The Homework turn is where you turn in homework and break his shields.
Using Draw during Homework gives you a Copy card, which is itself punished.
He says 'Gold Star' as flavor whenever you cast a spell directly at him.
Using the Copy card costs 15% max health to a Star Frostbite.
The second-position wizard loses 20% max health to a Star Insane Bolt if they fail to turn in homework, including if they cast Copy.
On the Lecture turn each wizard picks a self buff/debuff study card.
Using Draw during the Lecture gives you Doze Off - do not play it.
Snack Break makes you automatically fail the next Pop Quiz, so skip it.
A pet-cast Conviction placed before this turn can block the lecture stun.
Passing the Lecture costs 30% max health to a Star Cyclops.
Casting Doze Off is punished twice - a Star Leprechaun (25%) plus a Star Cyclops (30%) for 55% total.
If two wizards both cast Doze Off, the punishment does not trigger.
For the Pop Quiz he puts a school Aura on you and asks for its opposite - answer with the opposing school card.
Using Draw during the Pop Quiz gives you a Cheat card, which he punishes.
A wrong quiz answer costs 40% max health to a Star Supernova.
A correct answer dispels the aura with no damage.
He removes the aura harmlessly with a Supernova when you answer correctly.
Playing the Cheat card gets you an Impede debuff.
Later in the fight he can interrupt with short two-round Homework cycles reusing the second polymorph stage.
These secondary cycles run the Homework stage for two rounds.
Unlike the primary cycle, he keeps his normal deck and attacks the team during the secondary cycle.
The invisible polymorph in this cycle ends at the close of the second round.
Use these secondary-cycle turns to break more of his shields.
Shadow Creature and Shadow Self charms are stripped when the polymorph lands, causing 4,000 backlash damage, so do not hold them into this cycle.
